# Stoic Techniques

**Technique 1: Morning Meditation. Prepare for challenges. Visualize Stoic responses.**

**Technique 2: Evening Review. Reflect on your day. What did you do well? What can improve?**

**Technique 3: The Pause. Before reacting, pause. Ask: is this within my control?**

**Case: The Potter. A potter is not angry at the clay that breaks. A Stoic is not angry at the world.**

**Case: The Ship. You cannot control the wind. You can adjust the sails.**

**Case: The Arrow. A Stoic is like an archer: aim well, then accept the result.**

**Case: The Alarm Clock. Your alarm clock is not your enemy. It is a reminder of your purpose.**
**Case: The Traffic. Traffic is not a personal offense. It is a fact of life. Accept it.**
**Case: The Insult. An insult only hurts if you let it. The Stoic response is indifference.**
**Case: The Loss. Losing something is an opportunity to practice non-attachment.**
**Case: The Negative Visualization. Imagine losing what you value. It makes you appreciate it more.**
**Case: The Contemplation of the Sage. Imagine what a wise person would do. Then do that.**
**Case: The Labels. Call things by their true names. Cinnamon is not luxurious. It is a seed from a bush.**
**Case: The Reversal. When you are angry at someone, imagine their perspective. It reduces anger.**
